channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Elizabeth Strout and Miranda July are among finalists for the Women's Prize for Fiction
item.description: LONDON (AP) — American authors Elizabeth Strout and Miranda July are among finalists announced Wednesday for the Women’s Prize for Fiction, alongside four debut novelists exploring the search for freedom in different countries and cultures.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Republicans win Florida special elections in Trump strongholds by narrower margins than in 2024
item.description: ORMOND BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Republicans Jimmy Patronis and Randy Fine won special elections Tuesday in two Florida congressional districts, bolstered by President Donald Trump’s endorsement to fill vacant seats in reliably Republican strongholds.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, KATE PAYNE and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man rescued from rubble in Myanmar's capital as civil war complicates relief efforts
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Rescue crews in Myanmar pulled a 26-year-old man out alive from the rubble of the capital city hotel where he worked early Wednesday, but most teams were finding only bodies five days after a massive earthquake hit the country.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Democratic-backed Susan Crawford wins Wisconsin Supreme Court seat, cementing liberal majority
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — The Democratic-backed candidate for Wisconsin Supreme Court defeated a challenger endorsed by President Donald Trump and billionaire Elon Musk on Tuesday, touting her victory as a win against powerful interests and cementing a liberal majority for at least three more years.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Israel’s military operation in Gaza Strip expanding to seize 'large areas,' defense minister says
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s military operation in the Gaza Strip is expanding to seize “large areas,” the defense minister said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Val Kilmer, 'Top Gun' and Batman star with an intense approach, dies at 65
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Val Kilmer, the brooding, versatile actor who played fan favorite Iceman in “Top Gun,” donned a voluminous cape as Batman in “Batman Forever” and portrayed Jim Morrison in “The Doors,” has died. He was 65.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY and ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Actor Val Kilmer dies at 65
item.description: Val Kilmer, a veteran actor known for his roles as Jim Morrison and Batman, has died on Tuesday, his family confirmed to the New York Times. He was 65. His daughter, Mercedes Kilmer, said her father died of pneumonia and was surrounded by family and friends.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, KTLA 5

item.title: Man called 'Scamdaddy' sentenced for defrauding Utah-based credit unions out of $450K
item.description: A man who called himself "Scamdaddy" was sentenced for his involvement in a scheme to defraud Utah credit unions out of more than $450,000.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: US judge orders Trump administration to restore legal aid to unaccompanied migrant children
item.description: A federal judge in California has ordered the Trump administration to temporarily restore legal aid to unaccompanied migrant children in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Janie Har, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: CBP releases March border crossing numbers, marking lowest to date ever recorded
item.description: The Trump administration reported that there were just 7,180 southwest border crossings during March, compared to the sometimes 7,000 daily crossings under Biden.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Booker sets record for longest Senate speech in history
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) on Tuesday set a new record for the longest floor speech in Senate history, having held the floor for 25 hours and 5 minutes to decry potential GOP spending cuts in their looming tax bill and policies put in place by the Trump administration. Booker, the No.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Dressing sold at Trader Joe's recalled over undeclared allergens
item.description: A California company has issued a voluntary recall for a dressing distributed to 18 states and sold under the Trader Joe's private label.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KTLA 5

item.title: Dog of captain who lost boats in Lahaina wildfire barks with delight during whale encounter
item.description: A Hawaii boat captain who rebuilt her whale-watching tour business after losing three boats in the deadly 2023 Lahaina wildfire captured iPhone footage her of golden retriever barking excitedly when a humpback swam near them over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Sinco Kelleher, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge rejects Trump administration’s bid to move Mahmoud Khalil’s legal case to Louisiana
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has ruled that the legal battle over Mahmoud Khalil’s deportation should continue to play out in New Jersey, rejecting the Trump administration’s bid to transfer the Columbia University protester’s case to Louisiana. In a written decision Tuesday, U.S.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An ‘administrative error’ sent a Maryland man to an El Salvador prison, ICE says
item.description: President Donald Trump’ s administration has acknowledged mistakenly deporting a Maryland man with protected legal status to a notorious El Salvador prison last month, but is arguing against returning him to the United States because of his alleged gang ties and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House cancels rest of votes for week after GOP floor rebellion
item.description: House Republican leaders on Tuesday canceled votes for the rest of the week after a band of GOP lawmakers staged a rebellion on the floor, bringing legislative action to a screeching halt. House Majority Whip Tom Emmer (R-Minn.) informed lawmakers the next vote in the House would be Monday evening.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Jury selection begins in Karen Read retrial over the death of her Boston police boyfriend
item.description: Read is accused of striking her boyfriend, Boston police officer John O’Keefe, with her SUV and leaving him to die in a snowstorm outside of a house party.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, WGN 9

item.title: San Diego migrant shelter closes doors with numbers plummeting after Trump immigration crackdown: report
item.description: A San Diego migrant shelter is reportedly closing its doors and reverting to its original purpose after the number of asylum seekers dramatically drops.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Watch: Potential albino pygmy killer whale spotted off Hawaii
item.description: Whale watchers around Hawaii are crossing their fingers for another glimpse of what could be an albino whale.
item.dc:creator: Alina Lee, KRON4

item.title: DOGE staffer who resigned over past social media posts reinstated with higher access: Filing
item.description: A Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) staffer who resigned over racist posts that resurfaced on social media last month was reinstated to oversee the slashing of waste, fraud and abuse in March under the agency led by Elon Musk, according to court filings.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration admits it wrongly deported man to prison in El Salvador
item.description: The Trump administration acknowledged late Monday that it had mistakenly deported a Salvadoran man protected from removal, sending him to a facility in El Salvador where they argue they are unable to secure his return.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ohio school district reverts to birth names, catching students, teachers off guard
item.description: Students at Columbus City Schools with preferred names in the district's system had their names reverted back to match their birth certificates.
item.dc:creator: Delaney Ruth, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge blocks release of some records related to deaths of Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa
item.description: A New Mexico judge has decided to block the release of public records showing the bodies of Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Cruz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rain turns to ice, cuts power in Great Lakes region while Tennessee braces for wild weather
item.description: Freezing rain brought down trees and power lines in Michigan and Wisconsin, cutting electricity for thousands of people Sunday in the upper Great Lakes region, while forecasters said severe weather was on its way to Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Utah woman receives 100 cancer treatments, living far beyond doctors' expectations
item.description: An 84-year-old Utah woman recently received her 100th cancer treatment, outliving doctors' expectations.
item.dc:creator: Yeonseung Kim, Fox 5

item.title: Planning to visit these national parks? You may need to make a reservation to get in
item.description: While hundreds of parks are free to enter, others use timed entry and other reservation methods to control crowds — but that also means you may need to secure your spot well in advance.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Deputies won't face charges in deadly 2022 shootout on California highway, prosecutors say
item.description: San Bernardino County sheriff's deputies involved in a 2022 shootout along a California freeway that killed a teenage girl and her fugitive father will not face any criminal charges, state Attorney General Rob Bonta announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Who is Trevor Milton, the startup founder and donor pardoned by Trump
item.description: Milton said Thursday in a post on social platform X that he spoke to Trump over the phone and that he signed his pardon of “innocence.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Wisconsin appeals court won't stop Musk's $1 million payments to voters after attorney general sues
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin appellate court denied the state Democratic attorney general's request to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters at a rally planned for Sunday, just two days before a closely contested Supreme Court election.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Passenger flight and Air Force jet diverted from potential collision at DC airport
item.description: A U.S. passenger flight preparing to leave the nation's capital and an incoming military jet received instructions to divert and prevent a possible collision, officials said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Long Island iced tea may not be from that Long Island
item.description: There's likely a very specific Long Island you think of when you hear "Long Island iced tea." You may, however, be wrong.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: RECALL: Thousands of pounds of Our Family coffee mislabeled
item.description: The FDA lists this as a class II recall, in which use or exposure to a product “may cause temporary or medically reversible adverse health consequences or where the probability of serious adverse health consequences is remote.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, WGN 9

item.title: Chicago VA nurses protest Trump cuts, warn of negative impact on veterans' care
item.description: With the expansion of coverage under the PACT Act, passed with bipartisan support, more veterans seek care.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Barnes, WGN 9

item.title: Wisconsin attorney general sues Elon Musk to block $1 million payment offers
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin’s Democratic attorney general asked a court on Friday to block billionaire Elon Musk from handing out $1 million checks to voters this weekend, just days before the state’s hotly contested Supreme Court race was to be decided.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jury from outside of Memphis will hear the case of 3 former officers charged in Tyre Nichols' death
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — A jury from outside of Memphis will be seated in the state court trial of three former police officers charged with second-degree murder in the beating death of Tyre Nichols after a traffic stop in 2023, a judge ruled Friday. Shelby County Judge James Jones Jr.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
